focus: whether the salary of the job offer will be good
cardReadingDirection: Yes — the salary looks good, with both cards pointing to real material comfort rather than a lowball offer. Card 1 (Nine of Pentacles) = the pay itself will be solid and earned, but it comes with strain attached — expect the number to be tied to a demanding workload, so the user should confirm exactly what scope of work the salary covers and set a firm minimum figure before saying yes. Card 2 (The Empress) = the compensation grows steadily rather than peaking on day one — the base offer may be moderate, but benefits, raises, and long-term package value are where the abundance sits, so evaluate the total package, not just the monthly number. Concrete step: today, look up the market salary range for this exact role and seniority in your area, write down your target number and your walk-away number, and prepare one specific ask (base bump or a benefit like remote days or a review at 6 months) to use the moment the offer arrives.
